title: PubId as hex(SHA-256(key)) rather than raw public key — correct privacy design
description: identity.rs: PubId is hex(SHA-256(verifying_key_bytes)), not the verifying key itself. This is one-way: peers cannot reconstruct the signing key from the pub_id. The verifying key is sent separately only during auth (pub_key_b64 in Authenticate). A contributor might suggest using the raw base64 key as the pub_id for simplicity. Do not do that: (1) the hash adds a layer of indirection that allows future key rotation without changing the user's visible address, (2) if the key encoding ever changes (curve upgrade), the pub_id format stays stable as long as SHA-256 stays, (3) it prevents the relay from reconstructing the verifying key from the directory, reducing what the relay knows.
close_reason: informational review note — design confirmed correct, no action needed
